message:
perf(tui): lazy screen materialization, semantic db-stats diffing, and dashboard allocation reduction

Prevent hidden TUI screens from being instantiated by periodic
background ticks. Previously, screen_ids() reported ALL_SCREEN_IDS
regardless of whether screens had been visited, causing the runtime
tick strategy to create screen instances (with their workers and
side effects) before the operator ever navigated to them.

Key changes:

Screen lifecycle:
- Add materialized_screen_ids() returning only instantiated screens
- Add existing_mut() accessor that returns None for unmaterialized
  screens (vs get_mut which creates on access)
- screen_ids() now returns only materialized screens
- tick_screen() uses existing_mut() so ticking a hidden screen is
  a no-op rather than a materialization trigger

DB stats generation tracking:
- Add db_stats_semantically_changed() comparing all semantic fields
  but ignoring timestamp_micros
- db_stats_gen counter only increments on semantic content changes

Dashboard optimizations:
- cached_tool_latency_rows() returns Ref instead of Vec clone
- Extract detect_anomalies_from_samples() to accept pre-fetched data
- view() borrows current_db_stats by reference instead of cloning
- Remove fill_rect() per-cell clear loop
- Use into_iter() for rendered_lines to avoid String clones

Shared state helpers:
- Add tui_effects_enabled() and transport_mode_label() to avoid
  full config_snapshot() cloning in hot render paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
